qa_pairs.question: What is the primary function of the /archive filesystem?
qa_pairs.answer: The /archive filesystem, also referred to as $ARCHIVE, is a "nearline" storage pool intended for temporarily offloading semi-active material from other filesystems.

qa_pairs.question: When do users typically utilize the /archive filesystem?
qa_pairs.answer: Users typically offload or recall material from /archive as part of their regular workflow, or when they have reached their quotas on the /scratch or /project filesystems.

qa_pairs.question: How long can data be stored on HPSS when using the /archive filesystem?
qa_pairs.answer: Material stored through the /archive filesystem can remain on HPSS for a duration ranging from a few months to a few years.

qa_pairs.question: Is the /archive filesystem accessible to all users on Trillium?
qa_pairs.answer: No, on Trillium, the /archive filesystem is exclusively available to groups that have received a RAC allocation.
